mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales demonstrates strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 1-month change is positively correlated with 5-day SPX returns but Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal exhaustion of supply-side capacity and presage a growth deceleration that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as late-cycle expansion, negative INDPRO momentum fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates weakening residential investment and construction activity, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 4% annualized erodes real corporate margins and increases the probability of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 4% annualized is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 3%, simultaneous weakness in retail sales amplifies the drag from restrictive monetary policy, as both higher discount rates and lower consumer demand compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of FEDFUNDS above 3% and declining RSAFS does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1% annualized signals a near-stagnant economy where corporate revenue growth collapses, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth below 1% annualized is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.
